title: 4 Establishing Policies for FX Notes
body_text:
4	Establishing Policies for FX Notes
4.1 	Policy on Restricting Access to FX Notes
Your financial institution designates its own FX Notes users. Itâs recommended that you segregate duties and restrict administrative access to FX Notes. Practice a high level of due diligence when appointing FX Notes users. Make sure that access to the application reflects the usersâ current job responsibilities and authorization levels. See Section 5.2, FX Notes User Types.
4.2	Policy on Internal FX Notes Controls and User Management
Your financial institution is responsible for employing security practices and establishing internal controls for monitoring FX Notes activity. Itâs recommended that internal controls and procedures relating to the use of FX Notes are established to prevent or detect errors in a timely manner. Financial institution operations staff and external and internal auditors should pay particular attention to internal controls for FX Notes access.
Policies may include but are not limited to the following:
Ensuring that FX Notes users have their own unique user IDs and donât share user IDs
Ensuring that FX Notes users receive their one-time Client Centre password in a secure and confidential manner (applies to individuals who do not already have a Client Centre username or whose Client Centre password is reset)
Deleting FX Notes users who
â
are inactive for one year or more,
â
no longer require FX Notes access, or
â
are no longer employees of your financial institution
Ensuring that FX Notes users are reviewed on a regular basis and that required action is taken to update or delete FX Notes profiles.
4.3	Policy on Client Centre Passwords
FX Notes users access the application through a password login to the Client Centre. Secure passwords are necessary to prevent unauthorized access to the computer network. See the User Management Part for more information.
4.4	Policy on Internal Controls for Foreign Currency Orders
Your financial institution is responsible for security practices and internal controls. Operations staff and external and internal auditors must establish internal controls for ordering, selling, and maintaining an inventory of foreign currency.
Such policies can include but are not limited to the following:
Receiving and verifying foreign currency shipments in joint custody
Maintaining the vault register in joint custody
Monitoring the inventory of foreign currency to ensure that number of physical items corresponds to the vault register
Maintaining a log of foreign currency issued to each branch (if applicable)
Reconciling foreign currency orders daily to ensure shipments and deliveries are current
4.5	Policy on Complying Globex 2000 Rules and Restrictions
Establish internal training policies and procedures to ensure that FX Notes users comply with Globex 2000 rules and restrictions. Specifically, note the following:
Globex 2000 screens and monitors all payment transactions consistent with its legal and regulatory obligations.
Globex 2000 recommends that you consider if your financial institution has any reporting obligations (e.g., FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Reports) when a customer requests to divide payments for the same beneficiary on the same day under $10,000 (CAD equivalent).
END
